subtext: e.g., 'People buying gifts for iPhone-owning teens, in the US and UK.'
why: A clear picture of your ideal customer sharpens the market-size and marketing-channel research in your report.

text: What makes your product different from what's already for sale online?
why: What makes you different from existing sellers is central to whether your report can find a real opening in the market.

text: How do orders get made and shipped?
why: How orders get made and shipped drives your margins, lead times and how much cash you need up front.

option_notes.Print-on-demand: We'll focus on per-unit costs and margins, since there's no inventory risk or upfront stock to buy.
option_notes.Dropship from supplier: We'll look at supplier margins, shipping times and the risks of not controlling your own stock.
option_notes.Own inventory, ship from home: We'll factor in upfront stock costs, storage space and the time you'll spend packing orders.
option_notes.3PL warehouse: We'll factor in warehouse and pick-pack fees alongside your product costs and margins.
option_notes.White-label supplier + own storage: We'll cover supplier minimums, storage costs and the margin benefit of a branded product.

why: Where you sell changes your fee structure, how customers find you, and how much control you have over pricing and branding.

option_notes.Own Shopify store: We'll focus on platform and payment fees, and how you'll drive traffic without a marketplace's built-in audience.
option_notes.Etsy / marketplace only: We'll cover marketplace fees and how buyers discover and compare listings there.
option_notes.Amazon FBA: We'll factor in FBA storage and fulfilment fees, plus how Amazon's search and reviews affect visibility.
option_notes.Multiple channels (own site + marketplac: We'll cover fees and dynamics across each channel, since you're splitting sales between them.

text: What's your landed cost per unit from the supplier?
subtext: Include product + supplier shipping to you. Local currency. If you don't know yet, leave blank.
why: Your landed cost per unit is the base your margins are calculated from. Example figures here are just examples — your report prices everything in your own currency, using costs for your country.

text: How much does packaging cost per unit shipped?
subtext: Box or mailer + inserts + tape. Rough is fine. Leave blank for print-on-demand.
why: Packaging cost adds directly to your cost per unit. Example figures here are just examples — your report prices everything in your own currency, using costs for your country.

text: What retail price do you have in mind (per unit, local currency)?
why: Your target price, set against your costs, is what determines your margin. Example figures here are just examples — your report prices everything in your own currency, using costs for your country.
How widely you plan to ship changes your shipping costs, customs rules and how big a market we size for you.

option_notes.My country only: We'll keep shipping and compliance research local and skip cross-border customs and duties.
option_notes.My country + neighbouring countries: We'll factor in cross-border shipping costs and basic customs rules for nearby countries.
option_notes.English-speaking countries: We'll widen the market sizing and look at shipping costs and rules across those countries.
option_notes.Global (wherever the fulfilment supports: We'll size the market broadly and flag the customs and currency complexity of shipping worldwide.

text: Roughly how much money do you have to start?
subtext: For store setup, first ads, and any upfront stock or samples your model needs.
why: Your starting budget shapes what's realistic now versus later. Example figures here are just examples — your report prices everything in your own currency, using costs for your country.
